mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% signals an extended zero-lower-bound monetary regime. Sustained near-zero rates compress equity risk premiums and reduce discount rates on future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE at 8.6%) signals labor market weakness that triggers equity market pessimism. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, forward S&P 500 returns turn negative over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings pressure from reduced consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances (UNRATE +0.3pp in 3mo), or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 399,349M) and housing starts (HOUST at 628K) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity valuations expand as investors price in sustained earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase MoM
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 399,349M and HOUST at 628K as of 2011-10
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST grow MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 94.83) declining below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and capacity underutilization. This triggers negative forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward and reduce equity allocations in anticipation of margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances (INDPRO below 3mo MA), or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 131,708K) and unemployment rate (UNRATE at 8.6%) interact to signal labor market tightness. When PAYEMS grows month-over-month while UNRATE simultaneously declines, the dual signal indicates robust job creation that supports consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases MoM AND UNRATE decreases MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,708K and UNRATE at 8.6% as of 2011-11
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL at 226.76) rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy pressure and potential future rate increases. When CPI growth accelerates, equity investors demand higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as valuations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPI YoY > 2.5%, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 13,337.8B) growth rate below 2% annualized signals sub-trend economic expansion. When GDP growth decelerates below this threshold, equity investors reduce growth expectations and forward returns turn negative over 60 trading days as earnings revisions flow downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where GDPC1 annualized growth < 2%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal productive capacity and labor utilization. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, the dual signal indicates demand destruction and capacity underutilization, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings misses.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO decreases MoM AND PAYEMS decreases MoM
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.83 and PAYEMS at 131,708K as of 2011-11
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline MoM, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
